title: Incorrectly Implemented Greedy Coin Change Problem

statement.markdown: Given a target sum and an array of coin denominations, implement a function to find the minimum number of coins needed to make the target sum using the available coin denominations. The function should return the minimum number of coins required. If it's not possible to make the target sum, the function should return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a single line string containing a space separated array of integers representing the coin denominations, followed by the target sum on the next line.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the minimum number of coins required to make the target sum. If it's not possible to make the target sum, the output should be -1.
statement.notes: The coin denominations will be non-negative integers and the target sum will be a non-negative integer.

examples.explanation: The minimum number of coins required to make the target sum 10 is 2 (5 + 5).
It's not possible to make the target sum 7 using the available coin denominations.

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to solve the problem.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: We can use dynamic programming to solve the problem. The idea is to create a table where each cell represents the minimum number of coins required to make the target sum using the available coin denominations up to that index.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to try all possible combinations of coin denominations and calculate the minimum number of coins required.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution would be to use dynamic programming to solve the problem.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to create a table where each cell represents the minimum number of coins required to make the target sum using the available coin denominations up to that index. We can fill up the table using the following recurrence relation: minCoins[i] = min(minCoins[i-coin], minCoins[i-1]) + 1, where minCoins[i] represents the minimum number of coins required to make the target sum i using the available coin denominations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by induction.
editorial.dryRun: Here's a step-by-step example run of the optimal solution: 1. Initialize the table with -1. 2. Fill up the table using the recurrence relation. 3. Return the value of the last cell in the table.
commonMistakes: Not using dynamic programming to solve the problem.
Not filling up the table correctly using the recurrence relation.
